sentence: O tempo hoje não está nada bom.
ipa: ˈo ˈtẽpu ˈɔʒɨ ˈnɐ̃w̃ eˈʃta ˈnadɐ ˈbõ
gloss_en: The weather is not good at all today.
notes: Unstressed reduction [ˈɔʒɨ] and coda chiado [ʃ] in 'está' inherited unchanged from the base per mateus_dandrade2000; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'não' per cintra1971.

sentence: Os bilhetes custam quarenta euros.
ipa: ˈoʒ biˈʎɛtɨʃ ˈkuʃtɐ̃ kwɐˈɾẽtɐ ˈewɾuʃ
gloss_en: The tickets cost forty euros.
notes: Palatal [ʎ] in 'bilhetes' and coda [ʃ] in 'custam' per mateus_dandrade2000; 'Os bilhetes' voices the coda sibilant to [ʒ] before the voiced onset per pwl_coimbra.

sentence: A consulta é em fevereiro às nove horas.
ipa: ˈɐ kõˈsuɫtɐ ˈɛ ˈẽ fɨvɨˈɾejɾu ˈaʒ ˈnɔvɨ ˈɔɾɐʃ
gloss_en: The appointment is in February at nine.
notes: Coimbra keeps ⟨ei⟩ as [ej] in 'fevereiro' — intermediate between Lisbon [ɐj] and Porto [ej] — per pwl_coimbra; dark coda [ɫ] in 'consulta' and 'às nove' sibilant voicing to [ʒ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: A Filipa vive numa casa perto do rio.
ipa: ˈɐ fiˈlipɐ ˈvivɨ ˈnumɐ ˈkazɐ ˈpɛɾtu ˈdu ˈʁiu
gloss_en: Filipa lives in a house near the river.
notes: Coimbra retains the unstressed [i] that Lisbon cuts: 'Filipa' [fiˈlipɐ] not 'Flipa' per pwl_coimbra; strong word-initial [ʁ] in 'rio' inherited from the base per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Vira à esquerda e segue sempre em frente.
ipa: ˈviɾɐ ˈa eˈʃkɛɾdɐ ˈe ˈsɨɡɨ ˈsẽpɾɨ ˈẽ ˈfɾẽtɨ
gloss_en: Turn left and keep straight ahead.
notes: Coda [ʃ] in 'esquerda' and nasal [ẽ] in 'sempre'/'em' per mateus_dandrade2000; 'vira a' liaison in the central norm per cintra1971.

sentence: A sopa de peixe estava deliciosa.
ipa: ˈɐ ˈsɔpɐ ˈdɨ ˈpejʃ eˈʃtavɐ dɨlisiˈɔzɐ
gloss_en: The fish soup was delicious.
notes: ⟨ei⟩ kept as [ej] in 'peixe' and coda [ʃ] per pwl_coimbra; open-mid [ɔ] in 'sopa'/'deliciosa' inherited from the base per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Onde é que fica a estação dos comboios?
ipa: ˈõdɨ ˈɛ ˈkɨ ˈfikɐ ˈɐ eʃtɐˈsɐ̃w̃ ˈdɔʃ kõˈbojuʃ
gloss_en: Where is the train station?
notes: Nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'estação' and coda [ʃ] per mateus_dandrade2000; 'fica a' liaison in the neutral central variety per cintra1971.

sentence: Quantas pessoas vêm ao jantar?
ipa: ˈkwɐ̃tɐʃ pɨˈs̺ɔɐʒ ˈvẽ ˈɐu ʒɐ̃ˈtaɾ
gloss_en: How many people are coming to dinner?
notes: Nasal [ɐ̃]/[ẽ] in 'quantas'/'vêm' and coda [ʃ]/[ʒ] per mateus_dandrade2000; interrogative frame in the central norm per cintra1971.

sentence: Se eu pudesse, comprava aquela roupa.
ipa: ˈsɨ ˈew puˈdɛs̺ɨ kõˈpɾavɐ ɐˈkɛlɐ ˈʁopɐ
gloss_en: If I could, I would buy those clothes.
notes: Coimbra monophthongises ⟨ou⟩ to [o] like Lisbon and unlike the North: 'roupa' [ˈʁopɐ] per pwl_coimbra; conditional-imperfect frame and strong initial [ʁ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Ontem comprámos pouco pão na feira.
ipa: ˈõtɨ̃ kõˈpɾamuʃ ˈpoku ˈpɐ̃w̃ ˈnɐ ˈfejɾɐ
gloss_en: Yesterday we bought little bread at the market.
notes: ⟨ou⟩ monophthong [o] in 'pouco' beside kept ⟨ei⟩ [ej] in 'feira' per pwl_coimbra; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'pão' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Para o ano vamos viajar até ao Porto.
ipa: ˈpaɾɐ ˈo ˈɐnu ˈvamuʒ viɐˈʒaɾ ɐˈtɛ ˈɐu ˈpɔɾtu
gloss_en: Next year we will travel to Porto.
notes: 'até ao' vowel liaison and open-mid [ɔ] in 'Porto' per mateus_dandrade2000; 'vamos viajar' coda-sibilant voicing to [ʒ] per cintra1971.

sentence: Telefona-me quando saíres da piscina.
ipa: tɨlɨˈfɔnɐ ˈmɨ ˈkwɐ̃du sɐˈiɾɨʒ ˈdɐ piˈʃsinɐ
gloss_en: Call me when you get out of the pool.
notes: Coimbra retains the unstressed [i] Lisbon cuts: 'piscina' [piˈʃsinɐ] keeps its first [i] per pwl_coimbra; nasal [ɐ̃] in 'quando' and clitic imperative per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Boa tarde, então como tem passado?
ipa: ˈbɔɐ ˈtaɾd ẽˈtɐ̃w̃ ˈkɔmu ˈtẽ pɐˈs̺adu
gloss_en: Good afternoon, so how have you been?
notes: Nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'então' and nasal [ẽ] in 'tem' per mateus_dandrade2000; greeting register in the neutral central variety per cintra1971.

sentence: Quem não arrisca, não petisca.
ipa: ˈkɨ̃ ˈnɐ̃w̃ ɐˈʁiʃkɐ ˈnɐ̃w̃ pɨˈtiʃkɐ
gloss_en: Nothing ventured, nothing gained.
notes: Strong [ʁ] from ⟨rr⟩ in 'arrisca' and coda [ʃ] per mateus_dandrade2000; proverb kept in the central-norm register per cintra1971.

sentence: O coelho fugiu para trás do espelho.
ipa: ˈo kuˈɛʎu fuˈʒiw ˈpaɾɐ ˈtɾaʒ ˈdu eˈʃpɛʎu
gloss_en: The rabbit ran behind the mirror.
notes: Coimbra keeps the mid vowel before the palatal — 'coelho' [kuˈɛʎu], 'espelho' [eˈʃpɛʎu] — where Lisbon centralises to [ɐ] ('coâlho'), one of the sharpest Coimbra-Lisbon contrasts per pwl_coimbra; 'trás do' coda voicing to [ʒ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O vermelho daquela camisa é bonito.
ipa: ˈo vɨˈɾmɛʎu dɐˈkɛlɐ kɐˈmizɐ ˈɛ buˈnitu
gloss_en: The red of that shirt is pretty.
notes: Coimbra keeps stressed [ɛ] before the palatal in 'vermelho' [vɨˈɾmɛʎu] where Lisbon has 'vermalho' [ɐ] per pwl_coimbra; intervocalic [z] in 'camisa' and 'camisa é' liaison per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Os olhos dela brilham ao sol.
ipa: ˈoʒ ˈoʎuʒ ˈdɛlɐ ˈbɾiʎɐ̃ ˈɐu ˈsɔɫ
gloss_en: Her eyes shine in the sun.
notes: The marked Coimbra delta: word-final prevocalic ⟨s⟩ surfaces post-alveolar [ʒ] before a vowel — 'Os olhos' [ˈoʒ ˈoʎuʒ] — per pwl_coimbra; palatal [ʎ] and dark coda [ɫ] in 'sol' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O sol brilha por cima das colinas.
ipa: ˈo ˈsɔɫ ˈbɾiʎɐ ˈpɔɾ ˈsimɐ ˈdaʃ kuˈlinɐʃ
gloss_en: The sun shines over the hills.
notes: Dark coda [ɫ] in 'sol', palatal [ʎ] in 'brilha' and coda [ʃ] in 'das' inherited unchanged from the base per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: A disciplina exige muito trabalho.
ipa: ˈɐ diʃsiˈplinɐ eˈʃiʒɨ ˈmujtu tɾɐˈbaʎu
gloss_en: Discipline demands a lot of work.
notes: Coimbra retains the unstressed [i] Lisbon cuts in 'disciplina' [diʃsiˈplinɐ] per pwl_coimbra; palatal [ʎ] in 'trabalho' and coda [ʃ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: A velha ponte de pedra ainda aguenta.
ipa: ˈɐ ˈvɛʎɐ ˈpõtɨ ˈdɨ ˈpɛdɾɐ ˈaj̃dɐ ɐˈɡẽtɐ
gloss_en: The old stone bridge still holds.
notes: Palatal [ʎ] in 'velha' and nasal [õ] in 'ponte' per mateus_dandrade2000; open-mid [ɛ] kept in the central norm per cintra1971.
